title: Codex 2026 Prompting and Repository Practice · Mastery Check
summary: Apply current Codex guidance across CLI, IDE, app, planning, and repository instructions. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a Codex-specific prompt and AGENTS.md package. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use goal, context, constraints, and done conditions for material coding work.
Use Plan mode or an interview when requirements are fuzzy or choices are consequential.
Place recurring build, test, lint, review, and boundary guidance in the nearest applicable AGENTS.md.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.prompt:
# Cross-agent prompt portfolio
# Add tested contracts below. Keep shared intent separate from Claude and Codex adaptations.

steps.title: Reconstruct the defensible process
steps.instruction: Order the Codex Four Parts evidence cycle before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Codex Four Parts assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

content.text: Use goal, context, constraints, and done conditions for material coding work. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Check preconditions, perform the operation in an inspectable order, then verify the result and a boundary case.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Use a changed worked example case derived from this module: prompt codex practice. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. A defensible solution applies Codex Four Parts, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a Codex-specific prompt and AGENTS.md package.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Codex requires a rigid magic prompt syntax. This fails the Codex Four Parts contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

content.label: Research anchor
content.value: Anthropic Prompt Engineering Overview (Current 2026-07-13) — Controls success criteria, empirical evaluation, and the living Claude prompt-engineering workflow.

content.label: Mastery evidence
content.value: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use goal, context, constraints, and done conditions for material coding work.

hints: Name the Codex Four Parts precondition or invariant that the quiz case must preserve.
Trace this concrete case before choosing: Use a changed quiz case derived from this module: prompt codex practice. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: Codex requires a rigid magic prompt syntax.

options.text: Define the stakeholder decision, quantities, authority, and constraints
Choose and document the smallest defensible model or procedure
Execute the model while preserving inspectable intermediate evidence
Test boundaries and changed cases, communicate limits, and revise

options.text: Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.
Run the boundary case, inspect the actual mechanism, then accept or repair the claim.
Accept the result because visible output is the highest authority.

steps.title: Defend one difficult decision
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Codex Review Verify applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Prompt Codex to test, inspect the diff, review risk, and report evidence before completion.

checks.description: The evidence cycle runs from problem framing through changed-case validation.
checks.failureMessage: Frame the decision before modeling, and validate after obtaining a result.

checks.description: The decision applies Codex Four Parts to the changed stakeholder case.
checks.failureMessage: Recheck the conditions and reject the documented misconception about Codex Four Parts.
